content.full_text:
RAVI MEHTA
CHAIRMAN

FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION

November 8, 1995


Kathleen L. Burgess
County Counsel
County of Del Norte
586 "G" Street
Crescent City, CA 95531

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-95-334

Dear Ms. Burgess:

You have asked for advice concerning the conflict of interest
provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act"). The
Commission cannot offer specific advice regarding which employees
must be designated under a county's conflict-of-interest code.
The Commission is empowered to offer general advice and technical
assistance pursuant to Section 87312. Although we provide this
advice, please note that this advice is not binding on the county
or on the board as the county's code reviewing body.

QUESTION

Are the CDBG Housing Program Clerk I/II and the Housing
Program Clerk I/II making or participating in making governmental
decisions which could have a material financial effect upon those
positions and should those positions be included in the conflict
of interest code of the County of Del Norte?

ANSWER

The CDBG Housing Program Clerk I/II appears to be
"participating in making" governmental decisions which could have

_________________________
1
Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title
2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.


428 J STREET, SUITE 800, SACRAMENTO, CALIFORNIA 95814 (916) 322-5660

Burgess, Kathleen
File No. A-95-336
Page 2


a material financial effect upon that position and it should be
included in the county's conflict of interest code.  The Housing
Program Clerk I/II position does not make governmental decisions
and should not be included in the conflict of interest code.

FACTS

     The conflict of interest code for the County of Del Norte
includes within Appendix A the positions of "CDBG Program Clerk I/
II" and "Program Clerk I/II".  Your letter indicates that those
positions are assigned disclosure categories 3,4,6 and 8; however,
a review of the conflict of interest code indicates those
positions are assigned disclosure categories 3,4,7 and 8.
Appendix B requires disclosure of the following for those
categories:

     3.   Interests in real property.

     4.   Investments and business positions in, and income
          (including gifts or loans) from business entities
          or individuals who are subject to regulation,
          inspection or licensing by the County of Del Norte.

     7.   Investments and business positions in business
          entities and income from sources engaged in
          construction, building, or material supply.

     8.   Investments and business positions in business
          entities and income from sources engaged in
          construction, land development, or the acquisition
          or sale of real property.

     A review of the job description for the CDBG Housing Program
Clerk I/II indicates that this position presents applications to a
loan committee issuing housing rehabilitation loans.  The Program
Clerk estimates the cost of completing the requested repairs.  The
loans are approved by the loan committee.  The Program Clerk also
performs the following duties:

     Interviews parties applying for participation in the
     Housing Rehabilitation Program to obtain personal
     information required for the determination of
     eligibility.

     Presents all pertinent information regarding an
     applicant to the loan committee.

     Participates in initial inspection, detailing health
     and/or safety deficiencies and/or physical deterioration
     of the dwelling and electrical, plumbing and septic
     systems.

     Works with the Building Inspector in the preparation of
     work write ups and cost estimates.

Burgess, Kathleen
File No. A-95-336
Page 3


     Performs on-site review for compliance with the work
contract, change order, etc.

     Reviews and/or participates in the review of contractor
work to determine payment and requested payment for work
performed.

     A review of the job description of the Program Clerk I/II
indicates that this positions entails the following duties:

     Maintains departmental document files and binders.

     Processes employment documents.

     Maintains confidential records.

     Screens and distributes incoming correspondence and
responds to the more routine inquiries.

     Maintains health care benefit documents.

     Performs various tasks associated with employee
recruitment.

     Answers inquiries regarding job opportunities.

     Performs various special studies necessary to address
salary comparison, departmental reorganization, mandated
employment reports, etc.

     Performs clerical, secretarial and moderately technical
work of a confidential nature.

                              ANALYSIS

     The designation of employees under an agency's conflict-of-
interest code is left to the discretion of each individual agency
and its respective code reviewing body.  (Sections 87300-87310.)
Section 82011 indicates that the board of supervisors is the code
reviewing body with respect to the conflict of interest code of
any county agency, other than the board of supervisors, with
jurisdiction wholly within the county.  Therefore, as noted above,
the Commission cannot offer specific advice regarding which
employees must be designated under a county's conflict-of-interest
code.  The Commission is empowered to offer general advice and
technical assistance pursuant to Section 87312.  Although we
provide this advice, please note that this advice is not binding
on the county or on the board of supervisors as the county's code
reviewing body.

     The Act requires every agency to adopt and promulgate a
conflict of interest code (Section 87300).  A conflict of interest
code is a document which designates the positions within the

Burgess, Kathleen
File No. A-95-336
Page 4


agency which make or participate in the making of decisions which
may foreseeably have a material effect on any financial interest
and further sets forth the types of investments, business
positions, income and real property made reportable by those
designated employees (Section 87302).

Regulation 18700 provides guidance in determining whether a
public official is making or participating in the making a
governmental decision.

     a)    "Public official at any level of state or local
government" means every natural person who is a member,
officer, employee or consultant of a state or local
government agency...

          1)    "Member" shall include, but not be limited to,
salaried or unsalaried members of boards or
commissions with decision-making authority. A
board or commission possesses decision-making
authority whenever:

               A)    It may make a final governmental
decision;

               B)    It may compel a governmental decision; or
it may prevent a governmental decision either
by reason of an exclusive power to initiate
the decision or by reason of a veto which may
not be overridden; or

               C)    It makes substantive recommendations
which are, and over an extended period of time
have been, regularly approved without
significant amendment or modification by
another public official or governmental
agency...

          2)    "Consultant" means an individual who, pursuant
to a contract with a state or local government
agency:

               A)    Makes a governmental decision whether to:

                    1)    Approve a rate, rule or regulation;

                    2)    Adopt or enforce a law;

                    3)    Issue, deny, suspend, or revoke any
permit, license, application,
certificate, approval, order, or similar
authorization or entitlement;

                    4)    Authorize the agency to enter into,
modify, or renew a contract provided it

Burgess, Kathleen
File No. A-95-336
Page 5


                    is the type of contract which requires
                    agency approval;

                    5)   Grant agency approval to a contract
                    which requires agency approval and in
                    which the agency is a party or to the
                    specifications for such a contract;

                    6)   Grant agency approval to a plan,
                    design, report, study, or similar item;

                    7)   Adopt, or grant agency approval of,
                    policies, standards, or guidelines for
                    the agency, or for any subdivision
                    thereof; or...


     b)   A public official "makes a governmental decision,"
     when the official, acting within the authority of
     his or her office or position:

          1)   Votes on a matter;

          2)   Appoints a person;

          3)   Obligates or commits his or her agency to any
          course of action;

          4)   Enters into any contractual agreement on
          behalf of his or her agency;...

     c)   A public official "participates in making a
     governmental decision" ... when, acting within the
     authority of his or her position, the official:

          1)   Negotiates, without significant substantive
          review, with a governmental entity or private
          person regarding a governmental decision referenced
          in subdivision (a)(2)(A) above;

          2)   Advises or makes recommendations to the
          decisionmaker, either directly or without
          significant intervening substantive review, by;

               A)   Conducting research or making any
               investigation which requires the exercise of
               judgment on the part of the official and the
               purpose of which is to influence a
               governmental decision referenced in
               subdivision (a)(2)(A) above.

               B)   Preparing or presenting any report,
               analysis, or opinion, orally or in writing,
               which requires the exercise of judgement on

Burgess, Kathleen
File No. A-95-336
Page 6


                    the part of the official and the purpose of
                    which is to influence a governmental decision
                    referenced in subdivision (a)(2)(A) above.

        d)  Making or participating in the making of a
governmental decision shall not include:

                1)  Actions of public officials which are solely
                ministerial, secretarial, manual, or clerical;...

        From the information provided in the job description and job
announcement for the CDBG Housing Program Clerk I/II, we feel that
this position "participates in the making" of governmental
decisions as defined in Regulation 18700(C) and 18700(a)(2)(A)
when it presents, directly, a report to the loan committee
concerning the estimates in which require the exercise of judgment
concerning the total cost for completing repairs and the purpose
of the report is to influence a decision concerning the loan
application. It also appears that the CDBG Housing Program Clerk
I/II exercises judgement and is participating in the making of
governmental decisions when it "performs on-site review for
compliance with the work contract, change orders, etc." and
"reviews and/or participates in the review of contractor work to
determine payment and requested payment for work performed".
However, if this position does not exercise judgement and merely
uses objective standards in the course of their work without the
use of discretion it may not be participating in the making of
governmental decisions and would not have to be designated in the
code.

        A review of the job description for the Housing Program Clerk
I/II indicates that its duties are solely ministerial, secretarial
or clerical and does not exercise discretion or judgement, and
therefore does not make governmental decisions and should not be
included in your conflict of interest code.

        If you have additional questions, please contact me at (916)
322-5660.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        Steven G. Churchwell
                                        General Counsel

                                        By:  Wayne P. Imberi
                                             Political Reform Consultant
